finding: High unlock fee threshold is consistently negative in platform contexts
evidence: high_unlock_fee:platform_any is negative in 6/6 models; all market FE = -0.104 (p = 0.0009), all unit FE = -0.040 (p = 0.0003)
interpretation: The platform penalty is stronger as a threshold friction than as a purely linear fee effect: once access pricing crosses a salient level, platform membership is associated with lower capacity utilisation.
caveat: Threshold is based on the sample median of positive unlock fees and should be tested with alternative cutoffs.

finding: High unlock fee is especially detrimental for local MaaS and multi-platform contexts
evidence: high_unlock_fee:local_maas_only is negative in 6/6 models; all market FE = -0.150 (p = 0.002), all unit FE = -0.046 (p < 0.001). high_unlock_fee:multi_platform is negative in 6/6 models; all market FE = -0.131 (p = 0.0068), all unit FE = -0.098 (p < 0.001)
interpretation: The strongest version of the pricing-friction story is not simply any platform membership, but platform-mediated comparison environments where salient access fees become particularly costly.
caveat: Local MaaS and multi-platform exposure partly overlap with city-specific integration patterns.

finding: Subscriber-free-minutes promotions are positive for any platform and multi-platform exposure
evidence: promo_subscriber_minutes:platform_any is positive in 4/4 models; all market FE = 0.051 (p = 0.0006), all unit FE = 0.026 (p < 0.001). promo_subscriber_minutes:multi_platform is positive in 4/4 models; all market FE = 0.122 (p < 0.001), all unit FE = 0.043 (p < 0.001)
interpretation: Promotions that reduce marginal usage cost fit platform-mediated discovery particularly well, especially in multi-platform contexts.
caveat: Only 144 subscriber-free-minutes observations; use as mechanism probe, not standalone main claim.

finding: Subscriber-free-minutes promotions are negative in local MaaS contexts
evidence: promo_subscriber_minutes:local_maas_only is negative in 4/4 models; all market FE = -0.081 (p = 0.0035), all unit FE = -0.011 (p = 0.049)
interpretation: The same promotion format appears to work differently by platform architecture: broad multi-platform contexts amplify it, while local MaaS contexts do not.
caveat: Small promotion sample; may reflect one local campaign design rather than a general platform-type effect.

finding: Several local platforms show stronger price sensitivity than Free Now
evidence: price_per_minute_z:platform_KVV_regiomove_clean is negative in 4/4 models; all market FE = -0.091 (p < 0.001), all unit FE = -0.091 (p < 0.001). price_per_minute_z:platform_LeipzigMOVE_clean is negative in 4/4 models; all market FE = -0.065 (p < 0.001), all unit FE = -0.088 (p < 0.001)
interpretation: Platform effects are heterogeneous: some local MaaS integrations appear to make per-minute prices more salient, while Free Now does not dominate the pricing-friction result.
caveat: These are city-specific platform signals and should be framed as heterogeneity diagnostics.

finding: Transit-rich city contexts may intensify platform price sensitivity
evidence: price_per_minute_z:platform_any:PT_availability_z is negative in market FE for all (-0.043, p < 0.001) and no-weak exposure (-0.047, p < 0.001), but not unit FE
interpretation: Platform pricing appears more consequential where public transport alternatives are stronger, consistent with users comparing multimodal alternatives.
caveat: Not stable under unit fixed effects; needs a tighter city-context identification strategy.

finding: Wet-weather contexts modestly strengthen platform per-minute price sensitivity
evidence: price_per_minute_z:platform_any:share_weather_is_wet_z is negative in 3/4 models; all unit FE = -0.005 (p = 0.017), scooter market FE = -0.010 (p = 0.042), scooter unit FE = -0.012 (p = 0.011)
interpretation: When conditions are worse, platform users may become more selective about ongoing usage costs, particularly for scooters.
caveat: Effect size is small and should be treated as a supplementary boundary condition.

finding: Platform maturity may strengthen unlock-fee penalties within units
evidence: unlock_fee_z:platform_maturity_days_z is negative in unit FE for all (-0.169, p < 0.001) and scooter-only (-0.163, p < 0.001), but not market FE
interpretation: As platform membership matures, users may become more accustomed to comparison shopping, making unlock fees more punitive within the same city-operator unit.
caveat: Not visible in market FE; could reflect within-unit time dynamics rather than platform learning.
